Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchai Yojana (PMKSY) focuses on water use efficiency.
e-NAM platform aids farmers in marketing produce.
Soil Health Card Scheme promotes balanced fertilization.
ICAR plays a crucial role in agricultural research.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted impacts of climate change on Indian agriculture and the necessity for climate-resilient farming practices.
Discuss the efficacy of government schemes in promoting climate-smart agriculture and addressing the vulnerabilities of small and marginal farmers.
Evaluate the role of technology and traditional knowledge in building agricultural resilience against extreme weather events.
Suggest policy measures to further strengthen climate change adaptation and mitigation in India's agricultural sector.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with modern irrigation systems under changing weather conditions.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the world's largest agricultural producers, with agriculture contributing significantly to its GDP and employment.

GS3.headline: ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 Venus Orbiter Mission Enters Critical Design Review Phase
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced that its ambitious Shukrayaan-1 mission to Venus has successfully entered its Critical Design Review (CDR) phase.
• This milestone signifies the completion of detailed design work and a thorough assessment of the mission's technical readiness and scientific objectives.
• Shukrayaan-1 aims to study Venus's atmosphere, surface, and subsurface, utilizing advanced instruments developed by Indian and international partners.
• The mission is expected to provide crucial insights into Venus's runaway greenhouse effect and its implications for planetary habitability.
• Launch is anticipated in the late 2020s, with a window of opportunity opening every 19 months for an optimal trajectory to Venus.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
prelimsPoints: Shukrayaan-1 is India's first mission to Venus.
Expected launch period is late 2020s.
Objective: study Venus's atmosphere, surface, subsurface.
Instruments include a synthetic aperture radar and a ground-penetrating radar.
Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to similar size and mass.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of planetary exploration missions like Shukrayaan-1 for advancing scientific knowledge and India's capabilities in space technology.
Analyze the scientific objectives of Shukrayaan-1 and how its findings could contribute to understanding planetary evolution and climate change on Earth.
Evaluate the role of international collaboration in ISRO's deep-space missions and its benefits for global scientific community.
Examine the challenges involved in designing and executing missions to Venus, considering its extreme environment.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendition of the Shukrayaan-1 spacecraft orbiting Venus.
GS3.trivia: Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most other planets in our solar system, a phenomenon known as retrograde rotation.

GS3.headline: National Logistics Policy Driving Efficiency and Cost Reduction, Says Commerce Ministry
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry reported significant progress in the implementation of the National Logistics Policy (NLP), launched in 2022.
• The policy aims to reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP, which currently stands at 13-14%, to a global benchmark of 8%.
• Key achievements include improved multimodal connectivity, enhanced digital integration through platforms like ULIP (Unified Logistics Interface Platform), and streamlined regulatory processes.
• These efforts are contributing to increased competitiveness for Indian businesses, both domestically and internationally, by reducing transit times and operational costs.
• The government is also focusing on skill development in the logistics sector and promoting green logistics practices.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
Growth and development
prelimsPoints: National Logistics Policy (NLP) launched in September 2022.
Goal: reduce logistics cost to 8% of GDP.
PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan is foundational for NLP.
ULIP integrates digital services across logistics ecosystem.
Multimodal connectivity includes road, rail, air, and waterways.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and key pillars of the National Logistics Policy and its potential to transform India's logistics sector.
Discuss how improved logistics infrastructure and digital integration can enhance India's global competitiveness and boost exports.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving the target of reducing logistics costs and suggest measures to overcome them.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships and technology adoption in modernizing India's logistics ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating various modes of transport (truck, train, ship, plane) integrated into a logistics network.
GS3.trivia: The logistics sector in India is one of the largest employers, providing livelihoods to millions.

GS3.headline: India Expands Wetland Conservation Efforts with Designation of New Ramsar Sites
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) announced the addition of several new sites to the list of Wetlands of International Importance (Ramsar Sites) in India.
• This expansion underscores India's commitment to conserving its rich wetland biodiversity and fulfilling its obligations under the Ramsar Convention.
• Wetlands play a crucial role in maintaining ecological balance, providing critical ecosystem services such as water purification, flood control, and habitat for diverse flora and fauna.
• The new designations will bring increased attention and resources for the scientific management and sustainable utilization of these vital ecosystems.
• Local communities and stakeholders will be actively involved in the conservation plans to ensure their long-term viability.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Ramsar Convention signed in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971.
India joined the Ramsar Convention in 1982.
Chilika Lake was one of the first Ramsar sites in India.
Montreux Record is a register of Ramsar sites where changes in ecological character have occurred, are occurring, or are likely to occur.
Wetlands are transition zones between terrestrial and aquatic systems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Ramsar Convention and national policies in promoting wetland conservation and management in India.
Analyze the challenges in integrating local communities into wetland conservation efforts and suggest strategies for inclusive management.
Examine the impact of climate change on wetland ecosystems and the role of wetlands in climate change mitigation and adaptation.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and diverse vegetation.
GS3.trivia: World Wetlands Day is celebrated annually on February 2nd, marking the date of the adoption of the Convention on Wetlands in 1971.

GS3.headline: India Reiterates Support for Global Minimum Corporate Tax at G20 Finance Meeting
GS3.explainer:
• India reaffirmed its commitment to the global minimum corporate tax framework during the recent G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meeting.
• The framework, championed by the OECD, aims to ensure that multinational corporations pay a minimum effective tax rate of 15% on their profits, regardless of where they operate.
• India sees this as a crucial step towards curbing tax evasion, preventing profit shifting, and ensuring a fairer international tax system.
• Discussions focused on the timeline for implementation, addressing concerns of developing nations, and ensuring administrative simplicity.
• The move is expected to significantly increase tax revenues for many countries and promote greater tax equity globally.
gsSubTopics: Government Budgeting
Indian Economy
Effect of liberalization on the economy
International institutions
prelimsPoints: Global minimum corporate tax rate proposed is 15%.
OECD/G20 Inclusive Framework on BEPS developed the framework.
Pillar One deals with reallocation of taxing rights.
Pillar Two introduces global minimum tax.
Objective is to address challenges of taxing digital economy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the global minimum corporate tax and its potential impact on international tax regimes and corporate behavior.
Discuss the benefits and challenges for India in implementing the global minimum tax, considering its economic structure and investment climate.
Evaluate the role of G20 and OECD in shaping global economic governance and addressing issues like tax evasion and profit shifting.
Examine how the global minimum tax could affect India's tax revenue generation and its competitiveness as an investment destination.
GS3.imageDescription: A world map with currency symbols, representing global taxation.
GS3.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis, evolving into a key forum for international economic cooperation.

GS3.headline: Coastal Security Enhanced with New Surveillance Technologies and Inter-Agency Coordination
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence and the Ministry of Home Affairs announced a series of measures to further bolster India's coastal security apparatus.
• These include the deployment of advanced surveillance technologies, such as integrated coastal surveillance systems and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), along vulnerable stretches.
• Emphasis is being placed on strengthening inter-agency coordination between the Indian Navy, Coast Guard, Marine Police, and customs authorities for real-time intelligence sharing and joint operations.
• The initiatives aim to counter threats like maritime terrorism, smuggling, illegal fishing, and infiltration, safeguarding India's vast coastline and island territories.
• Community participation through coastal vigilance committees is also being promoted to create a multi-layered security framework.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
keyTerms: National Command Control Communication and Intelligence (NC3I) Network
prelimsPoints: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km.
Indian Coast Guard is the principal agency for coastal security in EEZ.
Marine Police operates in territorial waters.
IMAC at Gurugram integrates coastal surveillance data.
26/11 Mumbai attacks led to significant coastal security reforms.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted threats to India's coastal security and the evolution of its coastal defence strategy since 2008.
Discuss the challenges in achieving seamless coordination among various agencies involved in coastal security and suggest measures for improvement.
Evaluate the effectiveness of technological advancements and community involvement in strengthening coastal vigilance and deterrence.
Examine the geopolitical implications of maritime security challenges in the Indian Ocean Region for India's national interests.
GS3.imageDescription: A patrol boat of the Indian Coast Guard on a surveillance mission along the coastline.
GS3.trivia: India has 13 major ports and 200 non-major ports along its coastline.

GS3.headline: India Aims for Greater Role in Global Semiconductor Supply Chain with New Incentives
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced new incentives and policy adjustments to attract more investment in India's semiconductor ecosystem.
• The goal is to establish India as a significant player in the global semiconductor manufacturing and design supply chain, reducing reliance on imports.
• Focus areas include semiconductor fabrication units (fabs), display fabs, and design-linked incentive (DLI) schemes for chip design companies.
• This strategic push is critical for national security, economic growth, and supporting the 'Digital India' and 'Make in India' initiatives.
• The government is collaborating with academic institutions to build a skilled workforce necessary for the advanced semiconductor industry.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) is the nodal agency for this initiative.
PLI scheme for large-scale electronics manufacturing includes semiconductors.
DLI scheme supports chip design startups and MSMEs.
Semiconductors are crucial for almost all electronic devices.
Taiwan, South Korea, and the US are major players in global semiconductor manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the semiconductor industry for India's economic growth, national security, and technological sovereignty.
Discuss the challenges India faces in establishing a robust semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem and the policy measures being undertaken to overcome them.
Evaluate the potential of India to become a significant hub for semiconductor design and manufacturing in the global supply chain.
Examine the geopolitical implications of the global semiconductor shortage and India's efforts to build supply chain resilience.
GS3.imageDescription: A close-up of a silicon wafer with numerous microchips.
GS3.trivia: The first integrated circuit (chip) was invented by Jack Kilby and Robert Noyce independently in 1958.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Proposes National AI Ethics and Governance Framework
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog released a discussion paper outlining a proposed National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence (AI) Ethics and Governance Framework.
• The framework aims to guide the responsible development and deployment of AI technologies in India, ensuring fairness, transparency, accountability, and safety.
• Key principles include promoting human-centric AI, addressing algorithmic bias, ensuring data privacy, and establishing mechanisms for redressal.
• This initiative is crucial for fostering public trust in AI while harnessing its transformative potential across sectors like healthcare, education, and agriculture.
• Stakeholder consultations are being initiated to gather feedback from industry, academia, civil society, and government bodies before finalization.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is a policy think tank of the Government of India.
AI applications include natural language processing, computer vision, machine learning.
Ethical concerns: bias, privacy, accountability, job displacement.
India's National Strategy for AI (2018) 'AI for All' vision.
Responsible AI principles are globally debated.
mainsPoints: Analyze the ethical challenges and societal implications arising from the rapid development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence technologies in India.
Discuss the key components of NITI Aayog's proposed AI Ethics and Governance Framework and its potential to ensure responsible AI development.
Evaluate the balance between fostering innovation in AI and establishing robust regulatory mechanisms to address its risks.
Examine the role of government, industry, and civil society in building a trustworthy and human-centric AI ecosystem in India.
GS3.imageDescription: An abstract representation of AI, with human hands interacting with digital code and a brain icon.
GS3.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956 at the Dartmouth Conference.
